entries.details: Integrated Verification pass for AC-058 (regression vs AC-001..AC-050 on open-source layout). All core checks pass: npm install && npm run build -> dist/index.js; npx tsc --noEmit -> clean; docker build --platform linux/amd64 -> image runs as UID 10001; relay-config.docker.yaml parses via loadConfig() without code changes; no vendor SDKs in package.json or source tree; .env.example has zero vendor env vars.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-019 (health_check). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 health_check request through stub to relay. Response: [{resourceId:'order-pg', type:'postgres', healthy:true, latencyMs:6}, {resourceId:'order-mongo', type:'mongodb', healthy:true, latencyMs:0}]. All 5 test groups passed: correct JSON-RPC 2.0 response shape, both drivers present, all latencyMs >=0, error-handling path verified (try/catch in HealthReporter.checkAll() catches throws, logs warn with {err,resourceId}, returns healthy:false+latencyMs, other drivers unaffected), dispatch routes health_check to healthReporter.checkAll(). No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-017 (list_resources). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 list_resources request (no params) to relay through the stub. Response: { jsonrpc:'2.0', id, result: [{ resourceId:'order-pg', type:'postgres', name:'Order Service PostgreSQL', database:'relay', readOnly:true }, { resourceId:'order-mongo', type:'mongodb', name:'Order Service MongoDB', database:'relay', readOnly:true }] }. All 17 shape/contract checks passed: jsonrpc=2.0, id echoed, no error key, result is array of 2, every entry has exactly {resourceId,type,name,database,readOnly:true}, readOnly===true for all, types postgres|mongodb, both expected resources present, response shape matches createResponse(id,result). No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-018 (describe_resource). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). All 3 test cases pass: (1) unknown resourceId 'nonexistent' returns error code -32602 with message 'Unknown resource: nonexistent', (2) valid order-pg returns {tables:[{table_name,table_type}], type:'postgres', database:'relay'}, (3) valid order-mongo returns {tables:[], type:'mongodb', database:'relay'}. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-020 (protocol: unknown method + handler error). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 requests through stub to relay. (1) Unknown methods unknown_method, invalidMethod, foobar, delete all return error code -32601 with message Unknown method: <method>. (2) execute on non-existent table triggers catch block: code -32603, driver error message propagated, error logged with {err, requestId} in relay logs. (3) health_check still works after error (both drivers healthy). Source code confirms non-Error throws produce Internal error fallback. 10/10 checks passed. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-028 (postgres-sql-parser). validateQuery tested at compiled dist boundary. All three acceptance-check conditions verified: (1) validateQuery('SELECT 1') returns {valid:true}; (2) validateQuery('INSERT INTO t VALUES (1)') returns {valid:false, reason:'Only SELECT statements are allowed, got INSERT'} (parser-succeeds path) and the textual fallback 'INSERT statements are not allowed' works when the parser throws on malformed SQL; (3) validateQuery('SELECT 1; DROP TABLE users') returns {valid:false, reason:'Multi-statement queries are not allowed'}. Project builds cleanly (npm run build -> dist/). tsc --noEmit passes. PgDriver.validate() wires validateQuery for query/explain operations. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-022 (postgres-driver list_tables). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:'list_tables' against resource 'order-pg' through the stub to the relay. Response rows: [{table_name:'orders', table_type:'BASE TABLE'}], rowCount: 1, fields: [{name:'table_name', type:'text'}, {name:'table_type', type:'text'}]. The query SELECT table_name, table_type FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' ORDER BY table_name runs against the real relay-postgres container and returns exactly the expected shape. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-023 (postgres-driver describe_table). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:'describe_table', params:{tableName:'orders'} against resource 'order-pg' through the stub to the relay. Response rows: 5 column rows (id, status, customer, total_cents, created_at from information_schema.columns) + 1 constraint row (orders_pkey PRIMARY KEY on id tagged _type:'constraint' from information_schema.table_constraints). rowCount=6 matches rows.length. Column rows have column_name, data_type, is_nullable, column_default, character_maximum_length. Constraint rows have constraint_name, constraint_type, column_name, _type:'constraint'. The query runs information_schema.columns + information_schema.table_constraints scoped to public schema and orders table. 52/52 assertions passed. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-029 (postgres-sql-parser dangerous functions). validateQuery tested at compiled dist boundary. All 19 test cases pass: (1) validateQuery('SELECT pg_sleep(10)') => {valid:false, reason:'Dangerous function detected: pg_sleep'}; (2) validateQuery("SELECT * FROM pg_proc WHERE proname = 'pg_read_file'") => {valid:false, reason:'Dangerous function detected: pg_read_file'}; (3) all 15 documented dangerous functions (pg_sleep, lo_import, lo_export, pg_read_file, pg_write_file, pg_read_binary_file, pg_ls_dir, pg_stat_file, pg_terminate_backend, pg_cancel_backend, pg_reload_conf, set_config, current_setting, dblink, dblink_exec) are correctly rejected when their name appears anywhere in the lowercased query text, with the exact function name in the reason; (4) uppercase PG_SLEEP is also caught via lowercased text check; (5) dblink_exec has priority over dblink for correct function name; (6) safe query 'SELECT 1' still returns {valid:true}. Project builds cleanly (npm run build -> dist/). tsc --noEmit passes. PgDriver.validate() correctly wires validateQuery for query/explain operations. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-030 (postgres-sql-parser). Tested at compiled dist boundary. All three acceptance-check conditions verified: (1) validateQuery("WITH x AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM x") returns {valid:true} — parser.astify succeeds, stmt.type==="select", no dangerous functions found; (2) validateQuery("SELECT * FROM (SELECT pg_sleep(1)) s") returns {valid:false, reason:"Dangerous function detected: pg_sleep"} — parser.astify succeeds, text scan catches pg_sleep; (3) validateQuery("EXPLAIN SELECT 1") returns {valid:true} — parser throws on EXPLAIN, catch block fallback accepts via first-word EXPLAIN check. PgDriver.validate() delegates to validateQuery() for query/explain operations. npx tsc --noEmit passes. npm run build passes. No defect found.
